description: Fork merge-request code executes on a shared privileged runner with deploy credentials or host access.
body: Failure mode: a merge-request pipeline from a fork runs on a privileged runner (docker-in-docker, host volume mounts, or deploy-credentialed) because jobs are untagged or share the runner pool, letting attacker-controlled code escape the container or steal credentials. Detection signals: fork MR jobs landing on privileged/shared runners; untagged deploy-capable runners; no maintainer approval gate for fork pipelines. Do not run untrusted MR code on privileged runners. Safer fallback: tag jobs so untrusted pipelines use isolated minimal runners, reserve privileged runners for protected refs, and require maintainer approval to run fork MR pipelines.
